channel.title: DFIR Radar
channel.description: Keeping DFIR Intelligence on your Radar. The best DFIR research from hundreds of sources, filtered for relevance.

item.title: New: Use response actions to update Zscaler policies and block threats
item.description:
Red Canary launches automated Zscaler integration to instantly block malicious IOCs network-wide via response actions. Addresses social engineering attacks where valid credentials make threats look like legitimate user activity.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044280666804892112

item.title: CVE-2026-34621: Adobe Acrobat Reader Zero-Day Enables Arbitrary Code Execution via Crafted PDF
item.description:
CVE-2026-34621 zero-day in Adobe Acrobat/Reader enables arbitrary code execution via crafted PDFs. Adobe confirms active exploitation in the wild since late 2025. PoC now circulating on dark web forums. Patch immediately and hunt for util.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044265372694786123

item.title: Over 100 Chrome Web Store extensions steal user accounts, data
item.description:
Russian 🇷🇺 MaaS operation deploys 100+ malicious Chrome extensions stealing Google OAuth2 tokens and Telegram sessions via coordinated C2 infrastructure. Extensions remain live on Chrome Web Store despite disclosure.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044250266422165941

item.title: FTP Exposure Brief: Examining the 55-Year-Old Protocol Used by Millions
item.description:
Global FTP exposure drops 40% to 5.9M hosts since 2024, but 2.45M still lack TLS encryption. China 🇨🇳 (17.9%) and South Korea 🇰🇷 (14.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044235246065893705

item.title: Primer on GitHub Actions Security - Threat Model, Attacks and Defenses (Part 1/2)
item.description:
Comprehensive GitHub Actions security primer details three critical risks: pull_request_target misconfigurations enabling "Pwn Request" attacks, script injection via untrusted inputs, and compromised 3rd-party actions affecting 22K+ repos.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044220325521465504

item.title: Secure AI agent access patterns to AWS resources using Model Context Protocol
item.description:
New AWS guidance presents 3 security principles for securing AI agents accessing AWS resources via Model Context Protocol: assume all granted permissions can be used, provide organizational role governance, and differentiate AI-driven from huma...
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044204970510299189

item.title: Securing the Software Supply Chain: How SentinelOne’s AI EDR Autonomously Blocked the CPU-Z Watering Hole Cyber Attack
item.description:
Supply chain attack: CPUID's CPU-Z distribution compromised for 19 hours on April 9, delivering STX RAT through legitimate download infrastructure. Attackers compromised cpuid[.]com API to redirect downloads to malicious payloads bundled with signed binaries.
Key technical details: • Malicious CRYPTBASE.dll loaded via DLL search order hijacking, bypassing system DLL • Process chain: cpuz_x64.exe → powershell.exe → csc.exe → cvtres.exe (abnormal for CPU-Z) • STX RAT payload: reflective PE loader with XXTEA encryption, no disk artifacts • Triple persistence: registry Run key + 68-minute scheduled task + MSBuild .proj files in AppData\Local • C2: DNS-over-HTTPS to 1[.]1[.]1[.]1 resolving welcome[.]supp0v3[.]com, custom encrypted protocol
Attack infrastructure reused from March 2026 FileZilla campaign - same C2 domain supp0v3[.]com and IP 147[.]45[.]178[.]61. 150+ confirmed victims across IT sectors, likely initial access broker operation targeting privileged users.
Hunt for CRYPTBASE.dll outside C:\Windows\System32, monitor CPUID applications spawning PowerShell, and watch for DoH queries to 1[.]1[.]1[.]1/dns-query patterns.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044189818666561931

item.title: Omnistealer uses the blockchain to steal everything it can
item.description:
Omnistealer uses blockchain transactions on TRON, Aptos, and BSC to store encrypted staging code, creating undeletable C2 infrastructure. Targets 60+ crypto wallets, password managers, and cloud storage via fake coding gigs.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044174916417630215

item.title: Claude Code & ChatGPT Used to Steal Millions of Records in Mexican Government Breach
item.description:
AI-accelerated breach of 9 Mexican 🇲🇽 government orgs exposed 195M+ taxpayer records. Claude Code handled live exploitation while ChatGPT automated reconnaissance across 305 servers.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044159702922342445

item.title: Deep Dive into Architectural Vulnerabilities in Agentic LLM Browsers
item.description:
New research exposes critical attack vectors in agentic LLM browsers where XSS on trusted domains (perplexity.ai, openai.com) can hijack AI agents for full browser takeover.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044144409692852640

item.title: Digital Forensics: Analyzing Crashed ArduPilot Drones
item.description:
New methodology: Extracting flight data and mission plans from crashed ArduPilot drones reveals complete attack timelines, intended targets, and operational patterns for threat attribution and battlefield forensics.
Key technical details: • Two critical artifacts: .bin files (DataFlash logs with GPS, telemetry, timestamps) and .plan files (JSON waypoint missions showing intended routes) • Extract .bin logs from APM/LOGS/ directory on microSD card; recover .plan files via Mission Planner USB connection • Use mavlogdump.py to parse .bin files for takeoff coordinates, crash locations, speed profiles, and flight duration • QGroundControl visualizes .plan files showing spline waypoints, altitudes, hold patterns, and target areas
Analysis capabilities: • Cross-reference planned vs actual flight paths to identify deviations or mission failures • Extract precise crash coordinates, impact speed, and altitude from telemetry data • Calculate total flight time using takeoff/hit timestamps and TimeUS microsecond fields • Identify vehicle firmware, platform type, and configuration details
Methodology applies beyond aerial drones - ArduPilot runs on boats, submarines, ground rovers, making this valuable for diverse incident scenarios.
Parse recovered drone storage with mavlogdump.py and correlate .plan intentions against .bin reality for complete operational reconstruction.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044129539383799855

item.title: Mailbox Rule Abuse Emerges as Stealthy Post-Compromise Threat
item.description:
Microsoft 365 mailbox rule abuse surges as stealth persistence tactic — 10% of Q4 2025 breaches involved malicious rules created within seconds of compromise. Rules hide security alerts and forward data even after password resets.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044114523448324406

item.title: CPUID watering hole attack spreads STX RAT malware
item.description:
CPUID watering hole attack replaced CPU-Z and HWMonitor downloads with STX RAT for 6 hours on April 9-10. Attackers reused infrastructure from previous fake FileZilla campaign, enabling detection. Check DNS logs for vatrobran[.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044099109691199923

item.title: GraphQL RCE: The Kill Chain to Cloud Identity…!
item.description:
GraphQL sandbox escape via missing `__builtins__: {}` in exec() enables RCE → cloud service account takeover. Researcher bypassed Python sandbox using `__import__('os')` then extracted GCP OIDC tokens via metadata service.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044084334936658403

item.title: Low-Level Extraction for iOS 17 and 18
item.description:
ElcomSoft releases iOS Forensic Toolkit 10.0 with agent-based extraction now supporting iOS 17.0-17.7.8 and iOS 18.0-18.7.1, breaking through the previous iOS 16.6.1 barrier that had blocked low-level acquisition for months.
Key technical advances: • Agent targets A11-A18 SoCs (iPhone 8 through iPhone 16) using exploit chains for privilege escalation and sandbox escape • Covers remaining iOS 16.7-16.7.15 builds plus full iOS 17/18 branches for comprehensive keychain and filesystem access • A11 devices (iPhone 8/X) require agent method due to SEP patch blocking checkm8 if passcode ever configured • M-series iPads remain capped at iPadOS 16.6.1 pending future update
Certificate validation now requires internet connection for developer accounts created after June 6, 2021 🇺🇸 due to Apple's 🇺🇸 Provisioning Profile Queue requirements. iOS 17.3+ with Stolen Device Protection needs biometric auth for device trust.
Enhanced checkm8 support adds iOS 15.8.7, iPadOS 16.7.15, and iPadOS 18.7.5 coverage.
Route certificate validation through strict firewall allowing only PPQ traffic. Enable Developer Mode post-install on iOS 16+.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044069005506248924

item.title: When Trust Becomes a Weapon: Google Cloud Storage Phishing Deploying Remcos RAT
item.description:
New phishing campaign abuses legitimate Google Cloud Storage to deliver Remcos RAT via multi-stage JS/VBS/PowerShell chain, culminating in process hollowing of signed RegSvcs.exe binary.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044054027290169659

item.title: Hackers Exploit Kali Forms Vulnerability to Take Over WordPress Sites
item.description:
Critical RCE flaw in Kali Forms WordPress plugin (10K+ installs) actively exploited since March 20. Attackers use call_user_func() bypass to execute wp_set_auth_cookie for admin takeover. Update to v2.4.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044038676967989460

item.title: Digital Forensics: Extracting Secrets After a Cold Boot Attack
item.description:
New guide details cold boot attack methodology for RAM extraction from encrypted systems. Even locked machines with full disk encryption remain vulnerable to memory dumping via bootable USB drives on legacy BIOS systems.
Key technical details: • Attack exploits RAM data persistence after power loss - electrical charges fade gradually, not instantly • Uses bootable USB with GRUB4DOS + scraper64_haltonly.bin to dump physical memory to partition • Memory dump captured at /dev/sdb2, convertible via dd to ram.img for analysis • Volatility/Rekall frameworks reconstruct OS state: processes, registry hives, credentials, network connections
Attack methodology: • Force cold reboot via power cycling, hardware reset, or deliberate BSOD crash • Boot from USB bypasses OS memory clearing routines during normal shutdown • Scraper overwrites only ~820KB of RAM on systems with tens of GB - minimal forensic impact • Process takes time proportional to installed memory capacity
DFIR artifacts available: • Password hashes extractable from registry hives in memory • Domain credentials sometimes present in plaintext • Open files, clipboard contents, desktop screenshots may persist in graphical buffers • Full process listing and network connection state reconstruction possible
Important limitation: Modern EFI firmware writes random bytes across entire RAM during hardware initialization, effectively neutralizing this attack vector.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044023600420712743

item.title: Common Entra ID Security Assessment Findings – Part 4: Weak Conditional Access Policies
item.description:
Compass Security reveals critical gaps in Entra ID Conditional Access deployments that leave organizations vulnerable despite having policies in place. Common misconfigurations create exploitable bypasses for attackers with compromised credentials.
Key vulnerabilities identified: • Incomplete user targeting: MFA policies scoped to groups often miss users, leaving accounts unprotected. Role-based targeting fails with scoped assignments (Administrative Units not included in role conditions) • Resource targeting gaps: "Microsoft Admin Portals" protection doesn't cover Graph API access. Attackers can bypass portal restrictions using ROPC flow with first-party OAuth clients like Modern Workplace Customer API Native • Missing security info registration policies: Attackers with passwords can register their own MFA factors before general MFA policies activate, effectively bypassing multi-factor requirements • Built-in bypasses: Microsoft Intune Company Portal client (9ba1a5c7-f17a-4de9-a1f1-6178c8d51223) can bypass compliant device requirements while maintaining Graph API access with Device.Read.All permissions
Detection opportunities: • Monitor for Graph API tokens issued outside admin portals (check client_app_id in sign-in logs) • Alert on MFA registration from unusual locations/devices • Track ROPC authentication flows in audit logs • Review Conditional Access policy evaluation results for unexpected bypasses
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2044008523760115799

item.title: Fake Claude AI installer abuses DLL sideloading to deploy PlugX
item.description:
Fake Claude AI website distributes PlugX RAT via DLL sideloading attack. Campaign exploits AI tool popularity to deliver espionage malware through trojanized installer masquerading as Anthropic's chatbot service.
Technical details: • ZIP contains MSI installer dropping VBScript that launches legitimate Claude while copying NOVUpdate.exe, avk.dll, and encrypted .dat file to Startup folder • DLL sideloading (T1574.002) abuses legitimate G DATA antivirus updater to load malicious avk.dll • C2 communication to 8[.]217[.]190[.]58 over HTTPS within 22 seconds, hosted on Alibaba Cloud infrastructure • Self-deletion mechanism removes VBScript and batch file post-execution, leaving only sideloaded files and running process • Registry modification targeting TCP/IP settings to alter network behavior
Detection opportunities: • Monitor for NOVUpdate.exe with network connections from non-G DATA installations • Hunt for unsigned avk.dll files in Startup folders paired with legitimate signed executables • Alert on VBScript execution followed by rapid file deletion and HTTPS connections to cloud infrastructure
IOCs and detection signatures available in the Malwarebytes report.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2043993436894708121

item.title: Attackers Actively Exploiting Critical Vulnerability in Kali Forms Plugin
item.description:
Critical RCE in Kali Forms WordPress plugin actively exploited since patch disclosure. Attackers bypass authentication via function injection, escalate to admin access, then deploy malware through theme files.
Key technical details: • CVE-2026-3584 (CVSS 9.8) affects Kali Forms ≤2.4.9, patched in v2.4.10 • Vulnerability in prepare_post_data() function allows overwriting placeholders like {entryCounter} • Exploitation via call_user_func() enables execution of wp_set_auth_cookie(1) for admin bypass • POST to /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php with action=kaliforms_form_process and crafted entryCounter parameter
Attack progression: • Mass exploitation began March 20, 2026 (disclosure day) with 312K+ blocked attempts • Peak activity April 4-10 coinciding with free Wordfence protection rollout • Top attacking IPs: 209[.]146[.]60[.]26 (152K attempts), 49[.]156[.]40[.]126 (50K attempts) • Post-compromise: attackers edit functions.php to inject persistent malware
Hunt for POST requests to admin-ajax.php containing "kaliforms_form_process" action with suspicious entryCounter values. Review admin login logs for unusual authentication around March 20-April 10 timeframe.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2043978448276795631

item.title: JanelaRAT: a financial threat targeting users in Latin America
item.description:
JanelaRAT version 33 emerges as an evolved banking Trojan specifically targeting Brazilian 🇧🇷 and Mexican 🇲🇽 financial institutions with advanced session hijacking capabilities and daily C2 rotation.
Key technical details: • MSI dropper sideloads legitimate nevasca.exe + malicious PixelPaint.dll (JanelaRAT payload) • Protected with Eazfuscator obfuscation, uses AES encryption with MD5-derived keys • Dynamic C2 construction: concatenates obfuscated string + current date + DDNS suffix on port 443 • Targets 25+ Brazilian banks via hardcoded window title detection with 12-second delay • Full-screen overlay system mimics banking interfaces to harvest MFA tokens and credentials
Attack methodology: • Initial vector: phishing emails with fake invoice PDFs leading to malicious website downloads • Persistence via LNK shortcut in Startup folder pointing to renamed legitimate executable • User activity tracking with 10-minute inactivity monitoring for optimal remote operation timing • Real-time banking session manipulation including keystroke injection, mouse simulation, screenshot capture
DFIR artifacts: Monitor for nevasca.exe with network connections to DDNS services, LNK files in Startup folders, and PowerShell execution with PixelPaint.dll references. Hunt for processes checking banking software presence.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2043963182885024005

item.title: JitterDropper
item.description:
OALabs identifies JitterDropper, a Rust-compiled Windows dropper with distinctive API jitter patterns that acts as a fingerprint across nine builds spanning five weeks of active development.
Technical breakdown: • Two variants: Variant I embeds payload in .rdata with multi-pass decryption, Variant II downloads 122-byte shellcode from pixeldrain[.]com • Distinctive sleep jitter bounds per API: 60ms before InternetOpenA, 150ms before StringFromGUID2, 100ms before CoGetObject, 120ms before CoUninitialize • Anti-analysis features include CheckRemoteDebuggerPresent/IsDebuggerPresent pair, GUI cover window with no message pump, EnumWindows stall loop (21-27 iterations) • GetTickCount elapsed-check gates defeat sandboxes that compress time or skip Sleep calls - exits if delta exceeds threshold (e.g., 1153ms) • Delivers unknown .NET stealer and Vidar payloads via sRDI/Donut reflective loader
The 32-byte Lemire mod 1000 reduction pattern from rustc is compiler-generated, but the per-API jitter divisor table is unique to this malware family and enables reliable attribution across builds.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2043962204861423846

item.title: Phantom in the vault: Obsidian abused to deliver PhantomPulse RAT
item.description:
Novel campaign exploits Obsidian note-taking app to deliver PHANTOMPULSE RAT via weaponized community plugins. Cross-platform attack targets financial/crypto sectors through elaborate LinkedIn and Telegram social engineering.
Key technical details: • REF6598 campaign abuses Shell Commands and Hider plugins in shared Obsidian vaults for initial access • PHANTOMPULSE RAT features blockchain-based C2 resolution via Ethereum transaction data parsing from wallet 0xc117688c530b660e15085bF3A2B664117d8672aA • Windows chain: PHANTOMPULL loader uses AES-256-CBC decryption, timer queue callbacks, and reflective PE loading entirely in memory • macOS payload deploys obfuscated AppleScript with Telegram dead-drop fallback (t[.]me/ax03bot) • C2 weakness identified: anyone can hijack infrastructure by sending competing transactions to monitored wallet • IOCs: 195.3.222[.]251 staging server, panel.fefea22134[.]net C2 panel
Attack requires victim to manually enable community plugin sync after receiving shared vault credentials, then plugins auto-execute on vault open.
Hunt for child processes (PowerShell, bash) spawning from Obsidian with network connections. Monitor for obsidian-shellcommands directory creation and anomalous process execution from trusted applications. Full YARA rules and IOCs in report.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2043961641029775493

item.title: OT Reconnaissance with Wireshark and GrassMarlin
item.description:
Deep dive into Wireshark and GrassMarlin for OT network baselining reveals how attackers blend into normal traffic patterns.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2043321493363490943

item.title: Censys finds 5,219 devices exposed to attacks by Iranian APTs, majority in U.S.
item.description:
Censys identifies 5,219 internet-exposed Rockwell PLCs globally (74.6% in US 🇺🇸), vulnerable to Iranian 🇮🇷 APT attacks targeting critical infrastructure. Many run outdated firmware and additional services like VNC/Telnet.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2043072173632303326

item.title: GlassWorm evolves with Zig dropper to infect multiple developer tools
item.description:
GlassWorm campaign deploys Zig-compiled dropper via fake WakaTime extension to infect multiple IDEs (VS Code, Cursor, VSCodium) with second-stage RAT. Binary runs outside JavaScript sandbox for cross-IDE compromise.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2043011820970246218

item.title: When deleting Signal is not enough: the FBI, iPhone notifications, and what forensics can reveal
item.description:
FBI recovers Signal messages from iPhone notification database after app deletion. No encryption broken—iOS preserved incoming message content in system files, demonstrating critical gap between user assumptions and data persistence.
Key technical details: • Messages recovered from /private/var/mobile/Library/BulletinBoard/ notification database • Only incoming messages accessible (went through Apple Push Notification service) • Signal's "notification preview" setting enabled, storing plaintext in iOS system storage • Data persists in AFU (After First Unlock) state even after app uninstallation • Likely extracted via logical acquisition or encrypted iTunes backup analysis
Attack surface: • iOS BulletinBoard framework maintains notification history for system continuity • Push notification tokens remain valid briefly after app removal • Commercial tools like Cellebrite UFED can access notification artifacts in AFU state • iCloud backups may also contain notification database contents when legally compelled
DFIR implications: • Notification databases now confirmed high-value artifact for iOS investigations • Check ClearedSections.plist for notification clearing evidence • Cross-reference with interactionC.db and sysdiagnose logs for timeline reconstruction • Consider notification content settings during mobile device analysis
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042951348774183344

item.title: ClickFix finds a new way to infect Macs
item.description:
ClickFix campaigns now bypass macOS Terminal warnings by using applescript:// URLs to auto-launch Script Editor with pre-filled "maintenance" scripts that deploy Atomic Stealer. Users click "Apple prepared" buttons instead of pasting commands.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042785425329189192

item.title: CPUID hacked to deliver malware via CPU-Z, HWMonitor downloads
item.description:
CPUID compromised to deliver trojanized CPU-Z and HWMonitor downloads via poisoned API links. Multi-staged infostealer uses memory-only execution and EDR evasion. Same group hit FileZilla users last month.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042770179059327116

item.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly in Cybersecurity – Week 15
item.description:
DoJ disrupts Russian 🇷🇺 APT28 DNS hijacking network targeting thousands of TP-Link routers across 23 US 🇺🇸 states. Iranian 🇮🇷 APT escalates attacks on critical infrastructure PLCs via ports 44818, 2222, 102, 502.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042755186612146566

item.title: UAT-10362 linked to LucidRook attacks targeting Taiwan-based institutions
item.description:
UAT-10362 deploys LucidRook Lua-based stager against Taiwanese 🇹🇼 NGOs via spear-phishing with password-protected RAR archives. Malware uses DLL sideloading, geo-targeting for Traditional Chinese systems, and FTP C2 communication.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042739995912335705

item.title: Adobe Reader zero-day vulnerability in active exploitation
item.description:
Adobe Reader zero-day exploited since December 2025 via malicious PDFs executing obfuscated JavaScript. Russian 🇷🇺 oil/gas sector targeting observed. Disable Adobe Reader until patch available and scan PDF attachments.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042724994774450376

item.title: Just 21 IP Addresses Are Now Behind Nearly Half of All RDP Scanning on the Internet
item.description:
Just 21 IPs in Netherlands 🇳🇱 AS213438 generated 67% of global RDP scanning on April 7 — same ASN that went quiet in March then returned with burst-and-crash pattern. Check logs for auth attempts from these /24 blocks since April 5.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042709972304462190

item.title: How Phishing Kits Targeting U.S. Giants Are Built, Sold, and Deployed
item.description:
Phishing-as-a-Service ecosystem targeting US 🇺🇸 corporations has matured into commodity cybercrime with $120 AiTM kits bypassing MFA. Tycoon 2FA alone sent 30M+ phishing emails monthly to 500K+ organizations by mid-2025.
Key technical details: • Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) architecture captures authenticated session cookies post-MFA, not just credentials • Reverse proxy kits (Evilginx, EvilProxy) relay real-time traffic; application-driven models (Tycoon 2FA) clone login flows • Commercial pricing: Tycoon 2FA $120/10 days, EvilProxy $150-600/month, distributed via Telegram channels • Infrastructure abuse of trusted cloud providers: Cloudflare Pages incidents up 257% (460→1,370 cases 2023-2024) • Nation-state overlap: Russian 🇷🇺 Star Blizzard and Void Blizzard using same Evilginx tooling as cybercriminals
Attack chain: Phishing email → AiTM proxy/clone site → victim completes real MFA → session cookie harvested → account takeover. Scattered Spider leveraged this for $100M MGM Resorts breach.
Hunt for suspicious authentication patterns to trusted cloud domains and implement phishing-resistant FIDO2 rather than relying solely on traditional MFA.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042695415225815176

item.title: Inside Predator’s kernel engine
item.description:
Commercial spyware Predator achieves full iOS kernel access through ARM NEON vector registers and defeats Apple's Pointer Authentication with a sophisticated JavaScriptCore gadget hunt. Analysis reveals previously undocumented kernel exploitation engine.
Technical deep dive: • FDGuardNeonRW primitive channels 528 bytes per read through NEON V0-V31 registers via thread_get_state/thread_set_state Mach APIs • PAC bypass hunts 20-byte PACIA X16,X17 gadget in JSC::JSArrayBuffer::isShared() within JavaScriptCore framework • 256-entry pre-computed PAC signing cache enables microsecond hook callbacks without cryptographic latency • RWTransfer mechanism shares kernel R/W between processes via guarded file descriptors and Mach port manipulation • callFunc framework hijacks thread state through Mach exception messages for remote function execution • Supports 21 iPhone models (XS through 14 Pro Max) across 5 device classes with precise kernel offset configurations
Key artifacts: Exception messages with msgh_id 2406, trojan threads at breakpoints, TaskROPDevOff.h source references, and uname() XOR comparisons for device fingerprinting.
Hunt for unsigned threads with continuous Mach exceptions and JavaScriptCore dlopen/dlsym patterns in suspicious processes.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042679901828841849

item.title: Security’s Blind Spot: Physical Keyloggers That Bypass Antivirus Entirely
item.description:
NVISO Labs tested the AirDrive Forensic Keylogger Cable Pro, revealing how physical keyloggers create detection blind spots that completely bypass EDR systems. The device sits between keyboard and system as a USB HID passthrough, leaving zero OS-level artifacts.
Key findings: • Device creates WiFi hotspot (default SSID: AIR_XXYYZZ) for remote log access at 192[.]168[.]4[.]1 • USB power analysis shows distinctive current spikes (0.087A vs 0.007A baseline) during WiFi transmissions • Compatible only with standard office keyboards, not gaming keyboards with additional features • No processes, API calls, or network traces - operates as legitimate peripheral to OS • Investigation shifts from digital artifacts to physical access logs and camera footage
Detection approaches: • Rogue WiFi monitoring for suspicious SSIDs/BSSIDs in enterprise environments • USB power measurement shows periodic spikes but requires dedicated hardware • Physical inspections of cable runs, inline dongles behind monitors/docks • Asset management verification of keyboard models/serials vs records
Counter with strict physical access controls, FIDO2/WebAuthn passwordless MFA, tamper-evident seals on high-risk workstations, and continuous WLAN monitoring for unauthorized access points.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042664813332189347

item.title: Fake Claude site installs malware that gives attackers access to your computer
item.description:
Fake Claude AI site distributes PlugX malware via convincing installer. Attackers exploit Claude's 290M monthly visits, deploying trojanized "Claude-Pro" that works normally while establishing persistent backdoor access.
Campaign mechanics: • Fake site hosts Claude-Pro-windows-x64.zip with MSI installer to misspelled path C:\Program Files (x86)\Anthropic\Claude\Cluade\ • VBScript dropper (Claude.vbs) launches legitimate Claude app while copying PlugX triad to Startup folder • DLL sideloading via legitimate signed G DATA updater NOVUpdate.exe + malicious avk.dll + encrypted NOVUpdate.exe.dat (T1574.002) • C2 callback to 8[.]217[.]190[.]58:443 within 22 seconds, hosted on Alibaba Cloud infrastructure • Anti-forensic cleanup: dropper self-deletes via ~del.vbs.bat, errors suppressed with "On Error Resume Next"
This reuses February 2026 Lab52-documented technique with fresh AI lure. Hunt for NOVUpdate.exe in Startup folders and outbound connections to defanged IP. Full IOC hashes available in report.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042649597420384301

item.title: Dark Web Profile: TeamPCP
item.description:
TeamPCP executed 2026's most devastating supply chain attack, weaponizing security tools like Trivy and KICS to harvest 500,000 credentials from 1,000+ SaaS environments. The campaign cascaded across GitHub Actions, Docker, npm, PyPI, and OpenVSX ecosystems.
Key technical details: • Initial access via CVE-2026-33634 (CVSS 9.4) - incomplete token rotation after Feb 27 Pwn Request attack on Trivy CI/CD • Novel steganography: Python payloads embedded as base64 in WAV files with valid RIFF headers to evade detection • CanisterWorm: Self-propagating npm malware that auto-infected victim-maintained packages via stolen publish tokens • Imposter commits spoofed trusted contributor identities with backdated timestamps (T1036) • First documented abuse of Internet Computer Protocol (ICP) blockchain for C2: tdtqy-oyaaa-aaaae-af2dq-cai[.]raw[.]icp0[.]io
Attack methodology: • Credential harvesting via /proc/[pid]/mem scraping of GitHub Actions Runner.Worker processes • Fallback exfiltration: Created "tpcp-docs" repos in victim orgs, uploaded encrypted archives as release assets • Persistence: systemd services (pgmon.service) with Restart=always + privileged Kubernetes DaemonSets • Geopolitical targeting: Iran-specific wiper (rm -rf /) while deploying backdoors elsewhere
Major victims include European Commission 🇪🇺 AWS (92GB stolen), Aqua Security, Checkmarx, Palo Alto Networks, Telnyx, and 66+ npm packages. Campaign remains active with Vect Ransomware partnership announced.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042634581925400686

item.title: Microsoft: Canadian employees targeted in payroll pirate attacks
item.description:
Storm-2755 bypasses MFA using AiTM attacks to hijack Canadian 🇨🇦 employee payroll payments via fake Microsoft 365 sign-in pages.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042574138796110143

item.title: In my years of research, I’ve never seen this level of sophistication: Maltese-Localized DLL Side-loading & Advanced Domain Fronting
item.description:
Sophisticated campaign uses DLL side-loading via signed Microsoft binary with novel Maltese language evasion technique. Injected code forces Maltese MUI resources to poison telemetry and bypass localized security prompts.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042558998038106370

item.title: Claroty says CEA-852 adoption accelerates risk as building systems become exposed to critical infrastructure threats
item.description:
Claroty Team82 research reveals CEA-852 standard adoption exposes building management systems to critical vulnerabilities, with 75% of orgs running exploitable BMS and half exposing vulnerable systems online.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042543793673490711

item.title: SaaS Security Risks: Defending Against OAuth Abuse & Shadow
item.description:
Invictus IR outlines proactive Entra hardening: disable user consent entirely, enable admin consent workflow, and require explicit assignment for first-party apps to block OAuth abuse attacks.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042528799284433356

item.title: Microsoft Recall Flaw Exposes Decrypted User Data, Researchers Find
item.description:
Microsoft Recall security flaw allows standard user processes to extract decrypted screenshots and OCR data through DLL injection into unprotected AIXHost.exe component. Microsoft closed case as "Not a Vulnerability" despite proof-of-concept.
Technical details: • AIXHost.exe (timeline renderer) lacks PPL protection unlike aihost.exe, creating trust boundary gap • TotalRecall tool uses CreateToolhelp32Snapshot/VirtualAllocEx APIs for DLL injection - no admin required • Extracts full PNG screenshots, OCR text with pixel coordinates, URLs, timestamps via COM interfaces • Some functions like GetRecentCaptureThumbnail bypass Windows Hello authentication entirely • DiscardDataAccess patching maintains persistent access after Recall closes
Attack chain: • User authenticates via Windows Hello (natural or Win+J trigger) • Decrypted data flows through vulnerable AIXHost.exe process • Standard DLL injection embeds totalrecall_payload.dll • COM interface abuse extracts 90-day retention database (ukg.db) • Stealth mode patches access controls for indefinite persistence
DFIR artifacts: • Monitor process injection into AIXHost.exe via Sysmon EID 8 • Unusual DLL loads in %ProgramFiles%\WindowsApps\MicrosoftCorporationII.WindowsSubsystemForAndroid • COM object access patterns to Recall interfaces without user interaction • Modifications to ukg.db outside normal Recall operations
Hunt for CreateToolhelp32Snapshot calls targeting AIXHost.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042513772888142235

item.title: CI/CD Takeover & Supply Chain Risk! $$$$ Bounty
item.description:
Critical supply chain exposure in Linktree Android app - hardcoded secrets in strings.xml enabled CI/CD pipeline takeover and malicious OTA updates bypassing app store review. Check your mobile apps for hardcoded production keys.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042468345853710408

item.title: Atomic Stealer MacOS ClickFix Attack Bypasses Apple Security Warnings
item.description:
New Atomic Stealer campaign bypasses Apple's Terminal security warnings by shifting ClickFix attacks to Script Editor instead. Fake disk cleanup prompts trick users into pasting malicious commands that execute AMOS payload.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042453246107619500

item.title: UAT-10362 Targets Taiwanese NGOs with LucidRook Malware in Spear-Phishing Campaigns
item.description:
UAT-10362 targets Taiwanese 🇹🇼 NGOs with new Lua-based LucidRook malware via spear-phishing. Sophisticated stager embeds Lua interpreter and Rust-compiled libraries in DLL for payload delivery.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042438321910014315

item.title: New ‘LucidRook’ malware used in targeted attacks on NGOs, universities
item.description:
New LucidRook malware uses embedded Lua interpreter for modular payload delivery, targeting Taiwan 🇹🇼 NGOs and universities via spear-phishing. DLL sideloading technique complicates forensic analysis by hosting bytecode externally.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042423233236865460

item.title: From Tax Refund to Total Compromise: IRS-Themed Phishing Email Drives Full-Stack Financial Fraud
item.description:
New IRS-themed phishing campaign promises $5000 "tax refund courtesy of Elon Musk" then harvests credentials, photo IDs, and banking details through fake crypto platform.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042408327368622276

item.title: Google Warns of New Threat Group Targeting BPOs and Helpdesks
item.description:
New UNC6783 group targets BPO and helpdesk staff via fake Okta logins on spoofed zendesk-support[.]com domains, bypassing MFA through clipboard theft to enroll attacker devices.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042392829612142925

item.title: Elastic on Defence Cyber Marvel 2026: A Technical overview from the Exercise Floor
item.description:
Multi-national cyber exercise DCM26 demonstrates effective AI-powered defense with Elastic Security deployed for 40 Blue Teams across 29 countries.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042377934246191475

item.title: Tearing down a car telematic unit (and finding an accident on Facebook)
item.description:
Researchers extracted complete GPS logs from a BYD TCU, reconstructing the vehicle's entire journey from Chinese 🇨🇳 factory to UK 🇬🇧 operation to Polish 🇵🇱 salvage.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042362832076046830

item.title: What to Know About CyberAv3ngers: The IRGC-Linked Group Targeting Critical Infrastructure
item.description:
Iranian 🇮🇷 state group CyberAv3ngers escalates from PLC defacement to deploying custom ICS malware and exploiting unpatchable Rockwell CVE-2021-22681 across US 🇺🇸 critical infrastructure. Six-agency advisory confirms operational disruption and financial losses.
Technical evolution shows deliberate capability escalation: • Phase 1-2 (2020-2024): Default credential exploitation on 75+ Unitronics PLCs • Phase 3 (2024): IOCONTROL malware targeting Linux IoT/OT devices via MQTT over TLS (port 8883) • Phase 4 (2026): Active exploitation of CVE-2021-22681 (CVSS 9.8) authentication bypass in Rockwell Logix controllers
Key DFIR artifacts: • Network traffic on ports 44818, 2222, 102, 22, 502 from overseas VPS providers • MQTT over TLS and DNS-over-HTTPS from OT segments • Unauthorized Studio 5000 Logix Designer connections to PLCs • Modified PLC project files and manipulated HMI/SCADA displays
Critical impact factors: • No vendor patch available for CVE-2021-22681 - only defense-in-depth mitigations • Techniques proliferated to 60+ affiliated groups creating "swarm effect" • Confirmed targeting: water/wastewater, energy, government sectors
Immediate action: Disconnect internet-facing PLCs, set physical mode switches to "Run", implement secure gateways with MFA. Full IOC list in CISA AA26-097A.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042347593112404002

item.title: Malicious PDF reveals active Adobe Reader zero-day in the wild
item.description:
Active Adobe Reader zero-day exploits JavaScript engine to read local files via util.readFileIntoStream() API. Campaign targeting Russian 🇷🇺 oil/gas sector active for 4+ months with low AV detection (13/64). Patch urgently needed.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042332714485833770

item.title: Smart Slider updates hijacked to push malicious WordPress, Joomla versions
item.description:
Smart Slider 3 Pro update system compromised on April 7, pushing malicious WordPress/Joomla plugin with multiple backdoors, hidden admin accounts, and credential theft. Over 900K sites potentially affected. Restore backups from April 5 or earlier.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042317526714237294

item.title: Investigating Storm-2755: “Payroll pirate” attacks targeting Canadian employees
item.description:
Storm-2755 conducts "payroll pirate" attacks targeting Canadian 🇨🇦 employees using AiTM techniques to hijack authenticated sessions and redirect salary payments to attacker-controlled bank accounts.
Key technical details: • Initial access via SEO poisoning/malvertising on bluegraintours[.]com targeting "Office 365" searches • AiTM attack leverages Axios/1.7.9 user-agent to replay stolen session tokens, bypassing non-phishing resistant MFA • Error pattern: Multiple failed logins → 50199 sign-in interrupt → successful auth with user-agent shift to Axios • Persistence via non-interactive OfficeHome sign-ins every ~30 minutes for up to 30 days • Discovery focuses on "payroll", "HR", "finance" intranet searches and targeted phishing emails with subject "Question about direct deposit"
Defense evasion: • Creates inbox rules to hide emails containing "direct deposit" or "bank" keywords in conversation history • Operates during early morning hours (5:00 AM local time) to avoid detection • Manual manipulation of HR SaaS platforms like Workday to update banking information
Hunt for Axios user-agent strings with consistent session IDs following 50199 error codes, and monitor inbox rule creation with payroll-related keywords.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042302367169958238

item.title: In-Memory Loader Drops ScreenConnect
item.description:
Multi-stage in-memory loader chain abuses legitimate ScreenConnect RMM tool deployment. Attack uses heavy obfuscation, .NET reflection, and UAC bypass via auto-elevated COM objects to evade detection.
Technical breakdown: • VBScript loader (Acrobat_Reader_V112_6971.vbs) uses nested Replace() functions and Chr() calls to dynamically construct WScript.Shell objects and commands at runtime • PowerShell stage downloads C# source from drive.google[.]com, compiles entirely in-memory via Add-Type -ReferencedAssemblies to avoid disk artifacts • .NET loader performs PEB manipulation to masquerade as winhlp32.exe, splits method names ("Lo"+"ad"), uses Assembly.Load() for reflective loading (T1055.002) • UAC bypass leverages auto-elevated COM objects with reversed moniker strings, no user prompts required • Final payload downloads ScreenConnect.ClientSetup.msi from x0[.]at/qOfN.msi via msiexec installation
Hunt for PowerShell processes with -ExecutionPolicy Bypass downloading from cloud storage, followed by Add-Type compilation and suspicious winhlp32.exe network activity.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042287102763942088

item.title: Intent redirection vulnerability in third-party SDK exposed millions of Android wallets to potential risk
item.description:
Microsoft researchers discovered a severe intent redirection flaw in EngageLab's Android SDK affecting 50+ million app installations, with crypto wallets representing 30+ million at-risk users. Vulnerability allows sandbox bypass and unauthorized access to private app data.
Technical breakdown: • CVE affects EngageLab SDK versions prior to 5.2.1 - exported MTCommonActivity component in merged manifest creates attack surface • Malicious apps can craft intents with URI_ALLOW_UNSAFE flag to gain persistent read/write access to victim app's private storage via content providers • Vulnerability leverages trusted app context for privilege escalation (T1055) and data exfiltration from sandboxed environments • Attack chain: malicious intent → processIntent() → processPlatformMessage() → JSON parsing → startActivity() with elevated permissions • Primary impact on cryptocurrency/wallet apps due to sensitive financial data exposure
Fixed in EngageLab SDK v5.2.1 (Nov 2025). All vulnerable apps removed from Google Play.
Developers: audit merged Android manifests for unexpected exported components when integrating third-party SDKs. Review content provider permissions and validate intent handling code paths.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042272397860937825

item.title: How Phishing Is Targeting Germany’s Economy: Active Threats from Finance to Manufacturing
item.description:
German 🇩🇪 organizations across finance, healthcare, IT, telecom, and manufacturing face coordinated phishing campaigns using EvilProxy and FlowerStorm to bypass MFA via session hijacking. €178.6 billion in losses reported for 2024.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042256980530585901

item.title: Middle East Hack-for-Hire Operation Traced to South Asian Cyber Espionage Group
item.description:
South Asian Bitter APT group linked to hack-for-hire campaign targeting Middle Eastern journalists and civil society with ProSpy Android spyware. Successful Apple account takeovers completed in under 30 seconds via fake support impersonation.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042241810710356188

item.title: Censys warns systemic exposure of Rockwell PLCs enable Iran-linked targeting of critical infrastructure OT networks
item.description:
Iranian 🇮🇷 threat actors exploit 5,219 internet-exposed Rockwell PLCs globally using legitimate Studio 5000 software. Campaign targets US 🇺🇸 critical infrastructure via 'living off the land' techniques, achieving operational disruption without zero-days.
Key findings: • 74.6% of exposed devices in US 🇺🇸, with Spain 🇪🇸 (110), Taiwan 🇹🇼 (78), Italy 🇮🇹 (73) showing significant exposure • Primary targets: CompactLogix and MicroLogix 1400 families via EtherNet/IP port 44818 • 49.1% reach internet via Verizon cellular modems, 13.3% via AT&T - field-deployed infrastructure pattern • Multi-protocol reconnaissance: Modbus/502, Siemens S7/102, SSH/22 alongside EtherNet/IP • Exposed services: VNC (771), SSH (530), Telnet (280) enable HMI manipulation and SCADA display tampering
DFIR artifacts: Monitor TCP 44818, 2222, 102, 502, 22 for unexpected inbound connections. Hunt for Studio 5000 process execution on HMI workstations and unauthorized project file modifications.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042226781428564402

item.title: Russian Hackers Exploit SOHO Routers for DNS Hijacking Campaign
item.description:
Russian 🇷🇺 Forest Blizzard (Storm-2754) compromises 5,000+ SOHO routers for DNS hijacking, enabling passive surveillance of 200+ orgs and AiTM attacks against Microsoft 365. Deploy Zero Trust DNS and enforce MFA to mitigate.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042211578158657549

item.title: This fake Windows support website delivers password-stealing malware
item.description:
French-targeted 🇫🇷 infostealer masquerades as legitimate Windows update via typosquatted Microsoft domain. Multi-layered evasion using Electron wrapper, renamed Python interpreter, and obfuscated JavaScript achieves zero AV detections.
Technical breakdown: • Initial vector: microsoft-update[.]support serves fake KB update for Windows 24H2, downloads WindowsUpdate_1.0.0.msi (83MB) • Execution chain: MSI → Electron app (WindowsUpdate.exe) → VBS launcher (AppLauncher.vbs) → renamed Python interpreter (_winhost.exe) • Persistence: Registry Run key "SecurityHealth" + Startup folder shortcut "Spotify.lnk" for redundancy • C2 infrastructure: datawebsync-lvmv[.]onrender[.]com, sync-service[.]system-telemetry[.]workers[.]dev (Cloudflare Workers relay) • Data exfiltration via store8[.]gofile[.]io after killing 200+ processes with taskkill.exe
Campaign specifically targets France 🇫🇷 following massive breaches (Free ISP: 19M records, SFR telecom, France Travail: 43M records) providing attacker data for convincing social engineering.
Hunt for Electron processes spawning Python interpreters with suspicious names, outbound connections to .onrender.com/.workers.dev domains, and registry entries mimicking Windows services.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042196471802384602

item.title: The long road to your crypto: ClipBanker and its marathon infection chain
item.description:
ClipBanker campaign uses 11-stage infection chain starting from fake Proxifier software on GitHub. Complex fileless deployment evades detection through process injection and PowerShell obfuscation to steal cryptocurrency wallet addresses.
Technical breakdown: • Initial vector: Trojanized Proxifier installer on GitHub releases section with bundled legitimate software • Evasion: Creates Microsoft Defender exclusions via injected .NET modules (api_updater.exe, proxifierupdater.exe) • Persistence: Registry key at HKLM\SOFTWARE\System::Config stores Base64 PowerShell + scheduled task • Payload chain: Pastebin → GitHub → 500KB script → shellcode injection into fontdrvhost.exe • Final payload: C++ ClipBanker monitoring clipboard for 25+ crypto wallet formats (Bitcoin, Ethereum, Monero, etc.)
The malware uses fileless techniques with process hollowing (T1055) and PowerShell execution (T1059.001). It pings maper[.]info/2X5tF5 for infection confirmation. Over 2000 detections since early 2025, primarily in India 🇮🇳 and Vietnam 🇻🇳.
Hunt for scheduled tasks executing PowerShell with registry content, fontdrvhost.exe with network connections, and suspicious clipboard monitoring behavior. Full IOC list and URLs provided.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042181390720962579

item.title: UK NCSC says APT28 exploits routers for DNS hijacking, enabling large-scale traffic interception
item.description:
Russian 🇷🇺 APT28 (GRU Unit 26165) exploits vulnerable routers including TP-Link WR841N via CVE-2023-50224 to hijack DNS settings for adversary-in-the-middle attacks. Campaign targets email/login traffic for credential harvesting.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042166315398664318

item.title: ClickFix macOS Attack Uses Script Editor to Bypass Security Controls
item.description:
New ClickFix campaign bypasses macOS Terminal protections by using Script Editor instead. Apple-themed fake support pages trigger applescript:// URLs to deliver obfuscated Atomic Stealer via staged payload. Check logs for dryvecar[.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042121087090151894

item.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Update 007 - Cisco Source Code Stolen via Trivy-Linked Breach, Google GTIG Tracks TeamPCP as UNC6780, and CISA KEV Deadline Arrives with No Standalone Advisory, (Wed, Apr 8th)
item.description:
New reporting confirms TeamPCP (Google's 🇺🇸 UNC6780) used compromised Trivy scanner to steal Cisco source code as part of massive supply chain campaign affecting 1,000+ SaaS environments. CISA KEV deadline passed without standalone advisory.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042030550240702484

item.title: Hackers use pixel-large SVG trick to hide credit card stealer
item.description:
Massive campaign hits 100 Magento stores using pixel-sized SVG images to hide credit card skimmers.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042015279337906603

item.title: Unmasking Boggy Serpens' AI-Powered Surge
item.description:
Iranian 🇮🇷 Boggy Serpens (MuddyWater) uses AI to rapidly iterate Rust-based malware variants, compromising legitimate government accounts to bypass email filters. AI fingerprints found in LampoRAT code suggest automated development cycles.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2042000164165185872

item.title: Russia-linked APT28 uses PRISMEX to infiltrate Ukraine and allied infrastructure with advanced tactics
item.description:
Russian 🇷🇺 APT28 deploys PRISMEX malware against Ukraine 🇺🇦 and NATO allies using zero-day exploits CVE-2026-21509 and CVE-2026-21513. Campaign targets defense supply chains with steganography, COM hijacking, and Filen.io C2.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041985218635047056

item.title: New macOS stealer campaign uses Script Editor in ClickFix attack
item.description:
New Atomic Stealer campaign abuses macOS Script Editor via fake Apple cleanup guides, bypassing Terminal warnings in Tahoe 26.4. Malicious applescript:// URLs auto-launch obfuscated curl|zsh payloads. Treat Script Editor prompts as high-risk.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041970218298204332

item.title: A framework for securely collecting forensic artifacts into S3 buckets
item.description:
AWS Security Blog publishes comprehensive framework for securely collecting forensic artifacts into S3 buckets using least privilege access controls and time-limited credentials. Addresses critical security gaps in evidence collection workflows.
Key technical implementation: • IAM session policies + AWS STS AssumeRole API generate temporary credentials scoped to specific S3 object prefixes (e.g., CASE-0001/*) • ForensicsUploadRole with customer-managed KMS key encryption requires both S3 and KMS permissions for artifact access • Automated Step Functions workflow integrates with Systems Manager for remote collection from EC2 instances • Evidence bucket configured with CloudTrail data events, S3 versioning, and MFA delete protection • 1-hour default credential lifetime with programmatic credential vending via SQS/Lambda triggers
Architecture supports both AWS and non-AWS systems through existing forensic tools that accept AWS credentials. Session policies restrict uploads to case-specific prefixes while maintaining compatibility with third-party collection software.
Chain of custody preserved through comprehensive audit logging and immutable storage protections.
Deploy the AWS CDK solution from the collect-forensic-artifacts repository. Test credential scoping with: aws sts assume-role --policy restricting access to your case prefix.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041954929493889053

item.title: US operation evicts Russia from hacked SOHO routers used to breach critical infrastructure
item.description:
FBI Operation Masquerade evicts Russian 🇷🇺 GRU from compromised TP-Link SOHO routers used for DNS hijacking and AiTM attacks against US 🇺🇸 critical infrastructure. Verify DNS settings and update router firmware immediately.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041940092852273193

item.title: Node.js Trust Falls: Dangerous Module Resolution on Windows
item.description:
Node.js module resolution on Windows allows local privilege escalation when applications search C:\node_modules. Discord desktop app (CVE-2026-0776) remains unpatched - any user can plant malicious modules for code execution on app launch.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041924669712871578

item.title: Iranian-Affiliated APT Targeting of Rockwell/Allen-Bradley PLCs
item.description:
Iranian 🇮🇷 APT actors actively exploiting internet-facing Rockwell/Allen-Bradley PLCs using legitimate vendor tools. FBI/CISA joint advisory reveals 5,219 globally exposed devices with U.S. 🇺🇸 accounting for 75% of attack surface.
Key technical findings: • Actors using Studio 5000 Logix Designer for direct PLC access without zero-days (T1190) • CompactLogix and Micro850 families confirmed targeted via EtherNet/IP protocol • 49% of exposed PLCs reach internet via Verizon cellular modems - field-deployed infrastructure • Co-exposed services: VNC (771 instances), Telnet (280), Modbus (292) expand attack surface • CISA IOCs reveal single Windows workstation (DESKTOP-BOE5MUC) running full Rockwell toolchain across 185[.]82[.]73[.]160-171
Attack methodology: • Direct internet scanning for EIP/44818 responses from Rockwell devices • Legitimate software abuse for project file manipulation and HMI/SCADA display tampering • Multi-protocol probing (Modbus/502, S7/102) indicates broader OT targeting scope
Critical mitigations: Remove PLCs from direct internet exposure immediately. Set physical mode switches to RUN position on CompactLogix/MicroLogix. Hunt for inbound 44818 traffic from expanded IOC list in the report.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041909836196033004

item.title: FortiGate CVE-2025-59718 Exploitation: Incident Response Findings
item.description:
Rapid7 details real-world exploitation of FortiGate CVE-2025-59718, revealing how attackers used SSO bypass vulnerability to establish persistence and pivot into internal networks. Investigation showcases working backwards from internal compromise to edge device IAV.
Key findings: • CVE-2025-59718 exploited for SSO authentication bypass on FortiGate appliances - attackers created multiple admin accounts including forticloud.com domains • Attack progression: config download → account creation → SSL VPN enablement → internal network access via DHCP-assigned IPs • Post-exploitation: Mimikatz credential harvesting, lateral movement via PsExec/RDP, targeting of domain controllers and backup infrastructure • IOCs include Advanced_IP_Scanner, mimikatz.exe, and 11 malicious IPs (23.163.8[.]21, 45.32.216[.]250, others) • FortiGate logs show configuration changes, new firewall policies, and admin account creation from external IPs
Hunt for SSL VPN configuration changes from external IPs, new administrative accounts with suspicious domains, and unexpected DHCP assignments correlating with internal authentication events. Full IOC list and MITRE mappings in the report.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041894600923574760

item.title: Russian hacking group targets home and small office routers to spy on users
item.description:
Russian 🇷🇺 APT28 compromises SOHO routers to manipulate DNS settings, enabling credential theft and interception of Microsoft 365 traffic. Over 200 orgs and 5,000 devices affected. Check your router's DNS settings against ISP documentation.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041879473042165902

item.title: US Thwarts DNS Hijacking Network Controlled by Russian APT28 Hackers
item.description:
US 🇺🇸 authorities dismantle Russian 🇷🇺 APT28 DNS hijacking network spanning 23 states. Operation Masquerade used court-authorized commands to reset compromised TP-Link routers and restore legitimate DNS settings.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041864425783632130

item.title: Ongoing cyberattacks targeting internet-connected PLCs disrupt US critical infrastructure, agencies warn
item.description:
Iranian 🇮🇷 APT actors actively targeting internet-exposed Rockwell PLCs across US 🇺🇸 critical infrastructure since March, causing operational disruption in water, energy, and government sectors.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041849091559145977

item.title: New Lua-based malware “LucidRook” observed in targeted attacks against Taiwanese organizations
item.description:
Cisco Talos discovers LucidRook, a sophisticated Lua-based malware targeting Taiwanese 🇹🇼 NGOs and universities. Uses embedded Lua interpreter with Rust libraries for stealthy payload execution.
Key technical details: • Two infection chains: LNK-based (LucidPawn dropper) and EXE-based masquerading as Trend Micro antivirus • LucidRook embeds Lua 5.4.8 interpreter, downloads encrypted Lua bytecode from compromised FTP servers • Geo-targeting anti-analysis: only executes on Traditional Chinese systems (LANGID 0x0404/0x0C04) • String obfuscation uses runtime XOR decryption with parallel lookup tables • LucidKnight companion tool exfiltrates recon data via Gmail (fexopuboriw972@gmail.com)
Attack methodology: • Initial access via spearphishing with password-protected archives • DLL sideloading via legitimate DISM executable (T1574.002) • Persistence through Startup folder LNK files • C2 communication over plaintext FTP to abused Taiwanese 🇹🇼 printing company servers • Multi-layer encryption: RSA + password-protected ZIP for data exfiltration
Detection opportunities: • Monitor for DISM.exe with unexpected network connections • Hunt for DismCore.dll in %APPDATA%\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\ • Track FTP connections to 1.34.253[.]131 and 59.124.71[.]242 • Alert on DNS queries to digimg[.]store OAST service
Hunt for unsigned DLLs masquerading as system components with embedded interpreters. Full IOC list and ClamAV signatures available in Talos report.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041834085245489555

item.title: Iran‑Backed Threat Actors Hit US CNI Providers via Internet‑Facing OT Assets
item.description:
Iranian 🇮🇷 APT targets US 🇺🇸 critical infrastructure via internet-facing Rockwell Automation PLCs, manipulating SCADA displays across water, energy, and government sectors.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041803797467734028

item.title: U.S. agencies alert: Iran-linked actors target critical infrastructure PLCs
item.description:
Iranian 🇮🇷 IRGC-linked CyberAv3ngers target internet-exposed Rockwell/Allen-Bradley PLCs across US 🇺🇸 critical infrastructure, manipulating project files and SCADA displays. Disconnect PLCs from internet immediately or deploy firewalls.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041788750301171926

item.title: FBI Takes Down APT28 Network Behind Global DNS Hijacking Attacks
item.description:
FBI disrupts Russian 🇷🇺 APT28's global DNS hijacking network targeting thousands of TP-Link routers. Operation Masquerade neutralized malicious DNS resolvers used for credential harvesting via adversary-in-the-middle attacks.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041773654443233417

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Breach U.S. Industrial Systems, Trigger Disruptions
item.description:
Iranian 🇮🇷 APT actively breaches internet-facing PLCs to disrupt industrial operations, not just steal data. Attackers modify logic files and SCADA displays in real-time across water, energy, and government sectors.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041758572250308730

item.title: Claude Code Packaging Error Remains a Lure in an Active Campaign: What Defenders Should Do
item.description:
Threat actors exploit Anthropic's Claude Code npm packaging error to distribute Vidar infostealer, GhostSocks proxy malware, and PureLog Stealer via supply chain attack.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041698227263541646

item.title: Introducing C2 Detection: Know When Your Edge Devices Are Calling Home to Attackers
item.description:
GreyNoise launches C2 Detection to identify compromised edge devices through outbound traffic analysis. New capability extracts callback IPs from exploit payloads and maps post-exploitation infrastructure with 3-stage severity framework.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041683231993483334

item.title: Android APK Analysis Tool: AI-Powered Static Malware Analysis in Your Terminal
item.description:
New Android APK analysis tool combines AI-powered static analysis with YARA, VirusTotal integration, and MITRE ATT&CK mapping. Generates targeted Frida hooks for dynamic follow-up.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041668247435800958

item.title: React2Shell vulnerability helps hackers steal credentials, AI platform keys and other sensitive data
item.description:
UAT-10608 exploits React2Shell vulnerability to deploy automated credential harvesting at scale, compromising 766+ servers globally.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041652966336184546

item.title: CVE-2026-35616 & CVE-2026-21643 – Fortinet FortiClientEMS: Overview & Takeaways
item.description:
Two critical Fortinet FortiClientEMS flaws (CVE-2026-35616 & CVE-2026-21643) under active exploitation enable unauthenticated RCE via API bypass and SQL injection. Apply hotfixes immediately or upgrade to 7.4.5+ for affected versions.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041637967882514555

item.title: Fast-moving Storm-1175 uses new exploits to breach networks and drop Medusa
item.description:
Chinese 🇨🇳 Storm-1175 weaponizes 16+ CVEs within days of disclosure, deploying Medusa ransomware in under 24 hours across US 🇺🇸, UK 🇬🇧, and Australian 🇦🇺 healthcare, finance, and education sectors. IOCs available in Microsoft report.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041622883143647738

item.title: Storm-1175 exploits web-facing systems to drive ransomware attacks across healthcare and services in US, UK, Australia
item.description:
Storm-1175 rapidly weaponizes N-day vulnerabilities in web-facing systems, deploying Medusa ransomware within 24 hours across healthcare, education, and finance sectors in US 🇺🇸, UK 🇬🇧, and Australia 🇦🇺.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041607721775198624

item.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
item.description:
Russian 🇷🇺 GRU hackers exploited vulnerable routers to mass-harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from 18,000+ networks. Forest Blizzard (APT28) used DNS hijacking to intercept OAuth tokens without deploying malware, affecting 200+ organizations.
Technical breakdown: • Targeted end-of-life SOHO routers (primarily MikroTik, TP-Link) using known vulnerabilities • Modified router DNS settings to redirect traffic through attacker-controlled servers • Intercepted OAuth tokens post-authentication, bypassing MFA entirely (T1557.001) • Peak activity December 2025: 18,000 routers, 5,000 consumer devices compromised • Primary targets: government ministries, law enforcement, third-party email providers
Attack chain: • Router exploitation → DNS server modification → network-wide DNS poisoning • Users authenticate normally to Microsoft services • Tokens automatically intercepted via adversary-in-the-middle on TLS connections • Direct account access without credential theft or phishing
DFIR artifacts: Monitor DNS server changes on edge devices, unusual OAuth token requests from unfamiliar IP ranges, and TLS certificate anomalies on Microsoft domains.
Hunt for recent DNS configuration changes on SOHO devices and correlate with suspicious OAuth activity in Microsoft 365 logs.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041592523177472286

item.title: Milking the last drop of Intego - Time for Windows to get its LPE
item.description:
Local Privilege Escalation in Intego Windows security software exploits file deletion feature to achieve SYSTEM privileges through Config.msi deletion primitive and directory junction abuse.
Technical details: • CVE pending - affects Intego 3.0.0.1 Windows version • IavService.exe (SYSTEM) deletes "duplicate" files without validating file types or reparse points • Vulnerable functions: IavFileDeleteEx_DeleteFileEx and IavFilesUtil_RemoveFile lack FILE_ATTRIBUTE_REPARSE_POINT checks • Time-of-check-time-of-use (TOCTOU) race condition between GetFileAttributesW() and DeleteFileW()
Attack chain: • Create duplicate files, initiate Intego optimization scan • Replace target file with NT object directory junction to \RPC Control during cleanup window • Create NT symlink at \RPC Control\deleteme2.txt → C:\Config.msi • std::filesystem::remove() fallback follows symlink, deletes Config.msi as SYSTEM • Standard Config.msi rollback technique drops HID.DLL for SYSTEM shell via osk.exe
Monitor for unexpected Config.msi deletion events and NT object manager operations by security software processes. Hunt for SYSTEM file operations on junction/symlink targets outside expected paths.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041577568529478080

item.title: SOHO router compromise leads to DNS hijacking and adversary-in-the-middle attacks
item.description:
Russian 🇷🇺 military intelligence unit Forest Blizzard compromises 200+ organizations and 5,000 consumer devices through large-scale SOHO router exploitation for DNS hijacking and adversary-in-the-middle attacks.
Campaign details: • Storm-2754 sub-group exploits vulnerable home/small office routers, modifies DNS settings to redirect traffic through actor-controlled resolvers using dnsmasq utility • TLS AiTM attacks target Microsoft Outlook web domains and government servers in Africa 🌍, intercepting plaintext traffic including emails when users ignore certificate warnings • Attack chain: Router compromise → DNS hijacking via DHCP manipulation → selective AiTM against high-value targets for intelligence collection • Threat actor presents invalid TLS certificates to victims, requiring user interaction to complete compromise • Campaign active since August 2025, impacts government, IT, telecom, and energy sectors
Hunt for unusual DNS setting modifications on Windows endpoints and correlate with risky sign-ins. Monitor CloudAppEvents for "MailItemsAccessed" actions from suspicious accounts.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041562317700071558

item.title: API Authentication Bypass in FortiClient EMS 7.4.5-7.4.6–CVE-2026-35616
item.description:
Critical authentication bypass in FortiClient EMS 7.4.5-7.4.6 allows complete endpoint fleet takeover through HTTP header spoofing. Bishop Fox confirms active exploitation in the wild.
• CVE-2026-35616 (no CVSS provided) bypasses certificate auth via spoofed X-SSL-CLIENT-VERIFY and X-SSL-CLIENT-CERT headers • Django middleware trusts user-controllable headers as equivalent to Apache mod_ssl WSGI variables • Certificate validation performs only DN string matching - no cryptographic signature verification required • Attacker gains authenticated API access to quarantine endpoints, rotate JWT secrets, download ZTNA keys, export vulnerability data • Affects complete endpoint management infrastructure - FortiClient EMS controls organization's entire endpoint fleet • 16 cert_chain_approved endpoints across 15 controllers exposed to unauthenticated access
Fortinet hotfix strips malicious headers at Apache layer via RequestHeader unset directives. Code-level fix expected in v7.4.7.
Hunt for unusual EMS API access patterns and verify patching status with Bishop Fox's non-destructive scanner tool.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041547418869887195

item.title: The Unknown Stealers: What’s Hidden Below the Radar
item.description:
New research reveals lesser-known stealers like Void, Datura, and Saturn are operating below detection thresholds while feeding logs to ransomware groups. Void uses Steam profiles as C2 resolvers and dynamic syscalls to evade EDR hooks.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041532223824822695

item.title: Building a Detection Foundation: Part 5 - Correlation in Practice
item.description:
TrustedSec's 5-part series concludes with practical correlation techniques, combining Windows Security events, PowerShell logs, and Sysmon data to build comprehensive detection capabilities across logon tracking, process execution, and network ...
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041517053136572684

item.title: APT28 exploit routers to enable DNS hijacking operations
item.description:
Russian 🇷🇺 APT28/GRU exploits SOHO routers for large-scale DNS hijacking campaign, targeting authentication credentials through adversary-in-the-middle attacks. NCSC UK attributes operations to Military Intelligence Unit 26165.
Campaign mechanics: • Exploited TP-Link WR841N routers via CVE-2023-50224 to modify DHCP DNS settings (T1584.008, T1588.006) • Compromised routers redirect DNS queries for email/login domains to actor-controlled infrastructure • Conducted AitM attacks against browser sessions and desktop applications to harvest passwords and OAuth tokens (T1557, T1586) • Opportunistic targeting with filtering at each stage to identify high-value intelligence targets
Infrastructure details: • Two distinct IP clusters containing 150+ malicious DNS servers and AitM infrastructure • Primary DNS set to malicious IPs, secondary to legitimate servers for stealth • Interactive operations against MikroTik routers in Ukraine 🇺🇦 for intelligence collection • Multiple router models targeted beyond TP-Link WR841N
Hunt for unexpected DNS server changes in DHCP configurations, monitor for authentication traffic to suspicious IPs, and audit router firmware versions against CVE-2023-50224. Full IOC list with 150+ malicious IPs in the advisory.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041501907273851005

item.title: What we learned about TEE security from auditing WhatsApp's Private Inference
item.description:
Trail of Bits audit of WhatsApp's TEE-based AI feature found 28 vulnerabilities including 8 critical flaws that could bypass privacy protections. Key lesson: every unmeasured input becomes an attack vector in TEE deployments.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041486747494711385

item.title: Storm-1175 Exploits Flaws in High-Velocity Medusa Attacks
item.description:
Storm-1175 exploits n-day and zero-day vulnerabilities within days of disclosure for rapid Medusa ransomware deployment.
#DFIR_Radar | View on X: https://x.com/DFIR_Radar/status/2041471596829831370
